desc: Granada sits between the Sierra Nevada and Andalusia's plains, combining the Alhambra's world-class Islamic heritage with hilltop viewpoints, cave-flamenco traditions, and one of Spain's strongest tapas cultures. Demand is highest in spring and autumn when temperatures are comfortable, while midsummer is hotter but still active for evening-led city breaks.
hls: Mirador viewpoints with Sierra Nevada backdrops

todo.name: Alhambra timed-entry visit
todo.desc: Reserve early for Nasrid Palaces, Generalife gardens, and Alcazaba fortress views over Granada.

todo.name: Albaicín neighborhood walk
todo.desc: Get lost in whitewashed lanes, cármenes, and small plazas in the old Moorish quarter.

todo.name: Sunset at Mirador de San Nicolás
todo.desc: Watch evening light hit the Alhambra with Sierra Nevada peaks behind it.

todo.name: Sacromonte cave flamenco show
todo.desc: Experience zambra-style performances in hillside cave venues tied to Roma traditions.

todo.name: Granada Cathedral and Royal Chapel
todo.desc: Visit key Catholic monuments linked to the Reconquista and Spain’s Catholic Monarchs.

todo.name: Tapas crawl around Centro
todo.desc: Move between bars where drinks are commonly paired with complimentary local tapas.

wifi.note: Reliable in central and Albaicín accommodations; steeper hillside properties can have weaker indoor mobile coverage and variable room-by-room Wi-Fi strength.

prac.fltNote: Direct Gatwick–Granada options are limited, so many UK itineraries use Málaga (AGP) plus onward bus/train transfer (~2 hours). Return fares commonly run ~£45–£110 off-peak, ~£110–£180 in shoulder demand, and ~£180+ around Easter, summer peaks, and major holiday windows.
prac.visa: Spain is in Schengen; UK and US passport holders can generally stay visa-free for up to 90 days in any rolling 180-day period.
alerts: Alhambra timed-entry inventory often sells out well ahead in spring/autumn; book before flights where possible.
Summer afternoons can be very hot on exposed uphill routes; schedule major walks early/late.
Sacromonte and Albaicín slopes are steep/cobbled — choose luggage-light stays or arrange taxi drop-offs.

sweet: Mid-April to June and September to October are the strongest all-round windows for comfort, crowds, and long sightseeing days.
